element_introduction_order.element: Dig Dug protagonist and digging mechanic
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns tunnel creation and navigation through dirt tiles
Core combat system; requires timing to connect with enemies and repeated button presses to inflate
Primary threat; move through tiles as ghostly eyes when invulnerable, become solid in empty spaces
Environmental hazard and secondary combat tool; rocks fall after tile beneath them is removed, destroy enemies on impact
Secondary enemy type; presents fire attack threat in addition to touch damage
Reward for dropping rock pairs; point values increase as player progresses
Last enemy moves upward through tiles and escapes off top of screen; increases time pressure

typical_relief_triggers: Rock crush eliminating multiple enemies at once
typical_tension_triggers: Rocks falling in confined tunnel spaces
Player caught between enemy and falling rock
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical: exploration and setup phase followed by combat encounters; pacing intensifies as enemies become faster and more aggressive in later stages; final enemy escape creates acute time pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus items appear in specific spatial configurations (rock pair drops); player can create varied tunnel networks to access different enemy positions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Enemies move through tiles as ghostly eyes when unable to reach player directly, suggesting hidden pathways and vulnerable moments

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic digging and air pump inflation against Pookas and Fygars; manageable enemy counts and speeds
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy counts and speeds; players develop proficiency with rock crushing multi-kill bonuses; stage-to-stage dirt color variations
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significantly faster and more aggressive enemies; complex stage layouts requiring strategic tunnel planning; stage 256 causes kill screen (integer overflow bug in most versions)
notable_spikes: Stage 256 (kill screen): unwinnable state with inescapable Pooka spawn
Progression into mid-stage territory: noticeable jump in enemy speed and aggression
Late stages with maximum enemy density: requires optimal tunnel planning and multi-kill execution

notable_level_patterns.description: Predefined underground tunnel network with distributed enemy spawn points; player carves additional tunnels through dirt to access enemies or escape threats
Successive stages maintain core mechanics while systematically increasing enemy count and speed until escape behavior becomes necessary
Later stages introduce visual dirt color changes without mechanical alterations; cosmetic progression signal
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid to late game stages (visual progression marker)

notable_level_patterns.description: Integer overflow bug creates unwinnable scenario with inescapable Pooka spawn; functions as hard ending for most versions
